title: rubus oblongellus
intro: Rubus oblongellus, also known as the Western Thimbleberry, is a species of bramble in the rose family, characterized by its small, edible fruit and thimble-shaped calyx. The medicinal properties of Rubus oblongellus are attributed to its high content of antioxidants, vitamins, and minerals, which have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The health benefits of consuming Rubus oblongellus include reducing inflammation, improving heart health, and supporting digestive health, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. Rubus oblongellus can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or jam, with the fruit and leaves often used in herbal preparations to promote overall well-being.
